mnemonics: SAND: Signal travels from Soma to Axon to Nerve endings to Dendrites of the next cell to describe the basic pathway.
keyPoints: A biological neuron receives input signals through its dendrites.
The cell body (soma) processes these signals.
If the combined input exceeds a certain threshold, the neuron fires an electrical impulse (action potential) down its axon.
The axon terminal releases neurotransmitters into the synapse to communicate with the dendrites of the next neuron.
commonMistakes: Thinking neurons are directly connected; they communicate chemically across a gap (synapse), not electrically through a direct link.
Believing the action potential strength varies; it is an all-or-nothing event whose frequency, not amplitude, encodes information.
Assuming information only travels from dendrite to axon; the process is unidirectional under normal circumstances.
